name: mantle-mcp
description: MCP server for AI-driven Mantle L2 development — chain reads, simulation, and unsigned transaction building
installCommand: git clone https://github.com/mantle-xyz/mantle-agent-scaffold && cd mantle-agent-scaffold && npm install && npm run build
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-mcp

MCP server exposing Mantle L2 chain tools to AI agents. Gas token is MNT, not ETH.

## Tools (19 total)

### Chain Operations
- `mantle_getChainInfo` — network metadata
- `mantle_getChainStatus` — live status

### Registry & Validation
- `mantle_resolveAddress` — verify contract addresses
- `mantle_validateAddress` — address format validation

### Account & Balances
- `mantle_getBalance` — native MNT balance
- `mantle_getTokenBalances` — ERC-20 balances
- `mantle_getAllowances` — spender allowances

### Token Functions
- `mantle_getTokenInfo` — token metadata
- `mantle_resolveToken` — token symbol → address
- `mantle_getTokenPrices` — current prices

### DeFi Read
- `mantle_getSwapQuote` — swap quotes
- `mantle_getPoolLiquidity` — pool data
- `mantle_getPoolOpportunities` — yield opportunities
- `mantle_getProtocolTvl` — protocol TVL
- `mantle_getLendingMarkets` — lending market data

### Indexer
- `mantle_querySubgraph` — GraphQL subgraph queries
- `mantle_queryIndexerSql` — SQL indexer queries

### Diagnostics
- `mantle_checkRpcHealth` — RPC health check
- `mantle_probeEndpoint` — endpoint verification

## Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/mantle-xyz/mantle-agent-scaffold
cd mantle-agent-scaffold
npm install
npm run build
```

MCP client config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mantle": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "MANTLE_MCP_TRANSPORT": "stdio",
        "MANTLE_RPC_URL": "https://rpc.mantle.xyz",
        "MANTLE_SEPOLIA_RPC_URL": "https://rpc.sepolia.mantle.xyz"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

name: mantle-agent-scaffold
description: Full development scaffold bundling the Mantle MCP server, CLI, and pinned skills for AI-driven Mantle L2 development
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-agent-scaffold

Packages three things together for external Mantle integrations: a pinned `skills/` checkout, the Mantle MCP server, and a local CLI.

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install
npm run skills:init
npm run build
MANTLE_MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio npm start
```

## What's Included

- **MCP server** — 19 read-oriented Mantle tools over stdio
- **CLI** (`mantle-cli`) — same capabilities for shell workflows
- **Skills** — pinned checkout of `mantle-xyz/mantle-skills`

## Skills

```bash
npm run skills:init   # initialize pinned checkout
npm run skills:sync   # update to latest
```

## CLI Examples

```bash
node dist/cli/index.js chain info
node dist/cli/index.js registry resolve USDC --json
node dist/cli/index.js token prices --tokens USDC,WETH --json
```

## Documentation

[mantle-xyz.github.io/mantle-agent-scaffold](https://mantle-xyz.github.io/mantle-agent-scaffold/)

name: mantle-network-primer
description: Clarifies Mantle fundamentals — MNT gas, chain IDs, official endpoints, and inclusion vs settlement
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-network-primer

Onboarding and reference skill for Mantle network fundamentals.

## When to Use

- Starting Mantle development and need to confirm basics
- Clarifying MNT gas, chain IDs, or finality model
- Looking up official RPC endpoints or explorer links
- Answering "How is Mantle different from other chains?"

## Key Facts Covered

- Gas token is **MNT**, not ETH
- L2 inclusion ≠ L1 finality/settlement
- Mainnet chain ID: `5000`, Sepolia testnet: `5003`
- Public RPC has rate limits

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-network-primer
```

Or clone the full skills repo:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/mantle-xyz/mantle-skills
```

Then load `skills/mantle-network-primer/SKILL.md` into your agent context.

name: mantle-address-registry-navigator
description: Resolves verified Mantle addresses and blocks unsafe address guessing — anti-phishing safe lookup
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-address-registry-navigator

Registry and safety gate skill. Resolves verified Mantle contract and token addresses.

## When to Use

- Verifying a contract or token address before DeFi execution
- Anti-phishing checks on addresses received from external sources
- Looking up token addresses by symbol (e.g. USDT → 0x...)
- Fetching bridge, DEX router, or protocol addresses safely

## Behavior

- **Source priority**: MCP tools → local registry → BLOCKED
- Never guesses or generates addresses
- Returns `BLOCKED` if verification fails
- Asks for clarification when multiple candidates exist
- Marks deprecated/paused contracts as non-executable

## Confidence Levels

| Level | Source |
|-------|--------|
| `high` | Confirmed via MCP tool |
| `medium` | Local registry only |
| `low` | Freshness unknown |
| `none` | Not found |

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-address-registry-navigator
```

name: mantle-risk-evaluator
description: Returns pass/warn/block verdicts for state-changing intents — pre-execution safety gate
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-risk-evaluator

Pre-execution risk assessment skill for Mantle DeFi transactions.

## When to Use

- Before any swap, LP add/remove, or lending operation
- When you need slippage, gas, and address safety checked in one pass
- As the final safety gate before presenting a plan for user approval

## Checklist (5 items)

| Check | Warn | Block |
|-------|------|-------|
| Slippage | > 0.5% | > 1.0% |
| Price impact | > 2% | > 5% |
| Address safety | Unverified | Blacklisted |
| Allowance scope | Much larger than needed | Unlimited unconfirmed |
| Gas / deadline | > 20% above baseline | > 40% or > 60 min |

## Verdicts

- `pass` — all checks clear
- `warn` — review required, but can proceed
- `block` — execution must stop

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-risk-evaluator
```

name: mantle-portfolio-analyst
description: Inspects wallet balances, allowances, and spender exposure with read-only data
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-portfolio-analyst

Read-only wallet analysis skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- Checking MNT + ERC-20 balances for a wallet
- Auditing DeFi protocol allowances
- Identifying unlimited approvals (`critical` risk)
- Preparing for a security audit or allowance cleanup

## What It Reports

- Native MNT balance
- Token balances (symbol, address, raw + normalized)
- Allowance exposure with 4-level risk classification:
  - `low` — zero or within expected usage
  - `medium` — non-zero, bounded but larger than expected
  - `high` — significantly above expected
  - `critical` — unlimited (≥ 2^255)

## Tools Used

`mantle_getBalance`, `mantle_getTokenBalances`, `mantle_getAllowances`

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-portfolio-analyst
```

name: mantle-data-indexer
description: Retrieves historical wallet activity and time-windowed Mantle analytics via GraphQL/SQL
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-data-indexer

Historical data analytics skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- Looking up past swap or transaction history for a wallet
- Time-series analysis ("what was DEX volume in the last 7 days?")
- Protocol TVL, fee, or event aggregate data
- Questions that require GraphQL subgraph or SQL indexer queries

## Behavior

- Converts relative time ("last 7 days") to absolute UTC timestamps
- Uses `mantle_querySubgraph` (GraphQL) or `mantle_queryIndexerSql` (SQL)
- Never guesses endpoint URLs — returns `BLOCKED` if none provided
- Handles pagination and unit normalization
- Discloses indexer lag when present

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-data-indexer
```

name: mantle-readonly-debugger
description: Triages RPC failures, quote reverts, and ambiguous read-path behavior on Mantle
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-readonly-debugger

Read-path error triage skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- RPC call fails or connection drops
- `eth_call` reverts or quote API returns an error
- Balance looks wrong or nonce is unexpected
- Something failed before reaching the signing step

## Error Categories

1. RPC transport / connection errors
2. On-chain revert or call exception
3. Quote / liquidity failure
4. Balance / nonce mismatch
5. Other / unclassified

## Principles

- Preserves original error strings verbatim — no paraphrasing
- Proposes smallest reversible diagnostic steps first
- Provides bounded escalation checklist if unresolved

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-readonly-debugger
```

name: mantle-tx-simulator
description: Prepares external simulation handoffs and WYSIWYS summaries before transaction signing
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-tx-simulator

Pre-signing transaction simulation skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- Previewing what a transaction will do before signing
- Getting a plain-language "What You See Is What You Sign" (WYSIWYS) summary
- Analyzing revert reasons or estimating gas
- Reviewing multi-step bundles (e.g. approve + swap)

## Behavior

- Generates handoff package for external simulation backend
- Always produces WYSIWYS summary (labeled `PRELIMINARY` if simulation pending)
- Sets `do_not_execute` flag with reason if simulation fails
- Does not execute transactions directly

## Output Includes

- State diff (assets in/out, approval changes, contract state)
- Execution estimates (gas, fees, slippage)
- WYSIWYS plain-language outcome

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-tx-simulator
```

name: mantle-defi-operator
description: Orchestrates venue discovery, comparison, and execution-ready DeFi planning for Mantle
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-defi-operator

DeFi planning orchestration skill for Mantle. Combines address verification, risk assessment, and portfolio analysis into a single execution-ready workflow.

## When to Use

- Planning a swap, LP add/remove, or lending operation end-to-end
- Comparing protocols to find the best venue
- Generating an execution-ready calldata + approval sequence

## Planning Modes

| Mode | Output |
|------|--------|
| `discovery_only` | Protocol suggestions and rationale only |
| `compare_only` | Verified registry keys, no hex addresses |
| `execution_ready` | Full calldata, approvals, sequencing |

## Orchestration Flow

1. `mantle-address-registry-navigator` — verify addresses
2. `mantle-risk-evaluator` — pre-execution verdict
3. `mantle-portfolio-analyst` — balance/allowance check
4. Generate execution handoff (signing by external wallet)

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-defi-operator
```

name: mantle-smart-contract-developer
description: Frames Mantle-specific contract design and deployment-readiness decisions
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-smart-contract-developer

Smart contract design skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- Designing contracts to deploy on Mantle
- Choosing OpenZeppelin patterns for access control or upgradeability
- Defining constructor parameters, external dependencies, and admin roles
- Preparing a deployment-readiness checklist

## Behavior

- Does **not** write Solidity code — routes to OpenZeppelin MCP
- Fails closed: requests clarification before proceeding if requirements are incomplete
- Prevents mainnet/testnet confusion
- Always outputs a **Mantle Contract Development Brief**

## Output Brief Includes

- Project goal, environment, contract set
- Critical dependencies and external addresses needed
- Access control and upgradeability model
- OpenZeppelin MCP requirements
- Testing and security review focus

## Handoff

Once design is finalized, hand off to `mantle-smart-contract-deployer`.

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-smart-contract-developer
```

name: mantle-smart-contract-deployer
description: Prepares deployment, verification, and external signer handoff steps for Mantle contracts
author: mantle-xyz
readme:
# mantle-smart-contract-deployer

Contract deployment and verification skill for Mantle.

## When to Use

- Deploying a built contract to Mantle mainnet or Sepolia testnet
- Estimating gas and deployment cost
- Handing off the deployment payload to an external signer
- Verifying source code on the Mantle explorer after deployment

## Chain IDs

| Network | Chain ID |
|---------|----------|
| Mainnet | `5000` |
| Sepolia testnet | `5003` |

## Behavior

- Confirms chain ID before any deployment step
- Does not proceed without complete constructor arguments
- Read-only agent: no signing or broadcasting
- Does not write Solidity — redirects to `mantle-smart-contract-developer`
- Restarts pre-deploy checks if bytecode hash changes

## Output Report Includes

- Contract name, environment, chain ID
- Deployment tx hash, address, block, gas used
- Verification status and explorer link
- ABI path and constructor args (encoded)

## Install

```bash
claude skills add mantle-smart-contract-deployer
```
